---
title: "Governance Rights: Fork, Exit, and Audit"
chapter_id: "governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "ethica_mechanica"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "alignment_field"
  - "ladon_manhattan"
  - "spinoza"
  - "uat"
  - "field_of_god_ai_constitution"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Governance Rights: Fork, Exit, and Audit

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `ethica_mechanica`, `coherence_exchange`, `alignment_field`, `ladon_manhattan`, `spinoza`, `uat`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Powerful AI infrastructure needs rights and mechanisms that prevent governance lock-in and hidden control.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Policy statements about transparency are weak if users cannot audit, contest, exit, or fork the system in practice.

## Core Claim

[governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Fork, exit, audit, dissent, and contestability should be treated as technical governance interfaces, not only political ideals.

## Mechanism

- Represent rights as capabilities and required interfaces.
- Attach audit artifacts to governance decisions.
- Treat fork/exit as preserved escape hatches against lock-in.

## Interfaces

- Governance issues rights.
- SCFs preserve rights across replacement.
- Execution logs evidence for audit.

## Invariants

- Audit records cannot be silently deleted.
- Exit paths remain materially usable.
- Fork rights do not bypass safety constraints.

## Failure modes

- Governance capture.
- Data hostage-taking.
- Opaque automated policy updates.

## Minimal implementation

A governance-rights table with required artifacts, authorities, and failure tests.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Audit-record availability test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Exit-path preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Fork-right safety test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `ethica_mechanica` | Ethica Mechanica | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Geometric-order philosophical/constitutional source. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. |
| `ladon_manhattan` | Ladon & The Manhattan Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Kernel-level security architecture for high-agency AI. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. |
| `field_of_god_ai_constitution` | Field of God AI Constitution | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Recovered in the Project Theseus repository. Constitutional alignment core for truth alignment, agency preservation, consent, non-domination, consciousness caution, least sufficient power, auditability, self-authorization limits, and runtime checks; use as source material only after source-note creation, not as proof or test evidence. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
